data.title: Key facts: Starbucks sells 60% China stake to Boyu; expands U.S. tips
data.description:
• Starbucks sold 60% of its China retail ops to Boyu; Starbucks keeps 40% and global brand/IP. About 8,000 company stores will convert to licensed stores; partners target up to ~20,000 China locations.123
  • SBUX to expand U.S. tipping options for mobile and register scans and launch a performance-based quarterly bonus for store teams; changes roll out in company-owned U.S. stores starting in July.4

data.title: Key facts: ServiceNow buys Armis $7.75B; Stifel cuts target to $135
data.description:
• ServiceNow is acquiring Armis for $7.75B and has bought Veza and Moveworks to boost AI, identity and security features, noting Moveworks will integrate for improved platform interactions.1
  • Stifel cut ServiceNow (NOW) price target from $180 to $135, kept Buy. Cited slow Q1, weaker U.S. federal spending, a $15M de-obligation; expects federal revenue to improve in Q2 2026.2

data.title: Key facts: Rome court orders Netflix refunds in Italy; Hastings $2.09B
data.description:
• Rome court found Netflix Italia price hikes 2017–Jan 2024 unlawful, ordered lower Italian subscription prices, refunds (est. €250–€500), notices published; Netflix plans to appeal; Italy users ~8M.1
  • Reed Hastings holds ~21.16M Netflix (NFLX) shares (~0.5% outstanding), valued near $2.09B. Netflix stock rose in 2026 following earlier volatility.2

data.title: Key facts: MS2027 AI;Azure compute;$10B Japan;Copilot meets;Top optics
data.description:
• Microsoft building in-house frontier AI model for 2027; will need heavy compute. CFO shifted R&D priority and guidance; Cowen says training demand will limit Azure growth until mid-2027.1
  • Microsoft said Copilot paid subscriptions largely met targets in the March quarter; commercial chief called customer competition a “dog fight,” stressing secure deployment and multi‑model access.2
  • Microsoft to invest $10B in Japan over four years to expand AI and Azure cloud GPU capacity, add data centers, boost cybersecurity, and train up to 1M AI engineers by 2029.345
  • Microsoft raised capex to build AI compute capacity, boosting spending on AI data centers and hardware as part of broader tech investment in AI infrastructure; traders watch capex and execution.6
  • Microsoft is the top buyer of Applied Optoelectronics' data‑center transceivers, fueling demand for 800G and 1.6T optics as hyperscalers boost AI data‑center purchases.7

data.title: Key facts: KKR to Report Q1 2026 Results May 5; Bids for Park Holidays
data.description:
• KKR & Co. Inc. announced it will release first-quarter 2026 financial results on May 5, 2026, before the opening of trading on the New York Stock Exchange.12
  • KKR is among bidders, alongside Aermont, competing to acquire Park Holidays, a UK holiday park operator, in an active auction process to finalize the transaction.3

data.title: Key facts: Intel names Aparna Bawa; Boise exits; buys Apollo 49% $14.2B; Brookfield may free $20-30B
data.description:
• Intel said Chief Legal Officer April Miller Boise will leave effective June 1, 2026; company gave no successor or transition details. Traders note potential governance and leadership risk.1
  • Intel to buy Apollo’s 49% stake in Ireland Fab 34 for $14.2B, funded by cash and ~$6.5B new debt; gives Intel full ownership and company says EPS accretion and credit improvement from 2027.2
  • Intel may exit Brookfield SCIP, potentially freeing $20–$30 billion in repurchase capacity if the partnership ends and cash flow is unlocked.3
  • Intel named Aparna Bawa as Chief Legal and People Officer effective May 8. She will lead legal, ethics, compliance and HR, reporting directly to CEO Lip-Bu Tan.4

data.title: Key facts: Esperion Sells Royalties to Athyrium; Modifies Credit Deal
data.description:
• Esperion sold royalties and milestone receivables to Athyrium, monetizing future Otsuka-partnered product income to fund its Corstasis acquisition and support ongoing operations.1
  • On April 2, 2026, Esperion (ESPR) amended its credit agreement, per an SEC filing, noting the date and change to its credit facility without disclosing financial or forward-looking details.2

data.title: Key facts: Citigroup $0.60 Dividend; CEO Pay $95.8M; Solvay <3%
data.description:
• Citigroup declared a $0.60 common quarterly dividend per share payable May 22, 2026; also declared preferred‑stock dividends. No further details on funding, policy changes, or future payouts.1
  • Citigroup filed with the SEC that CEO Jane Fraser’s total 2025 pay was $95.8 million, disclosed in the company’s regulatory filing for that fiscal year.2
  • Citigroup reported its direct voting stake in Solvay fell below 3% as of March 27, 2026, based on a 105,876,416 voting rights denominator.3

data.title: Key facts: Bunge Increases Securitization to $2.0B; 8-K on Obligation
data.description:
• Bunge raised its receivables securitization capacity by $500M to $2.0B, cut the accordion to $500M, adjusted pricing and margin, changed seller subsidiaries, and removed sustainability terms.123
  • On April 2, 2026 Bunge Global SA (BG) filed an 8-K disclosing a direct or off‑balance sheet financial obligation, noting Item 1.01 is incorporated by reference into Item 2.03.4

data.title: Key facts: Barclays Cuts Coinbase PT to $148; Clarity Act Markup Soon
data.description:
• Barclays cut Coinbase (COIN) price target to $148, citing need for higher trading volume and crypto prices. Notes diversification into subscriptions, USDC and custody; stock tied to crypto sentiment.1
  • Coinbase (COIN): Chief legal officer Paul Grewal said regulators and industry are near agreement on the Clarity Act and a markup hearing could occur within 48 hours, per company comment2

data.title: Key facts: AMZN ~$11B FCF '26; NLRB orders ALU bargain; 3.5% FBA fee
data.description:
• Amazon (AMZN) now expected to produce roughly $11B free cash flow in 2026 after cuts; heavy capex for AI compute and data centers is boosting spending while earnings stay high.1
  • NLRB found Amazon violated labor law at Staten Island center, ordered recognition and bargaining with Amazon Labor Union (~5,000–5,500 workers) and required employee notices; Amazon plans to appeal.234
  • Amazon (AMZN) will add a temporary 3.5% fuel and logistics surcharge to FBA, Buy with Prime and Multi‑Channel Fulfillment in the U.S. and Canada, effective April 17 and May 2 respectively.5

data.title: Skillsoft Corp expected to post earnings of $1.27 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Skillsoft Corp , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 7 for the period ending January 31 2026

  • The Nashua New Hampshire-based company is expected to report a 2.7% decrease in revenue to $130.15 million from $133.75 million a year ago, according to the estimate from one analyst, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Skillsoft Corp is for earnings of $1.27 per share.

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "hold".

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

data.title: Xcel Brands Inc expected to post a loss of 49 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Xcel Brands Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 7 for the period ending December 31 2025

  • The New York City-based company is expected to report a 35.8% increase in revenue to $1.642 million from $1.21 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Xcel Brands Inc is for a loss of 49 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Xcel Brands Inc is $5.00, about 270.4% above its last closing price of $1.35

data.title: CryptoQuant Issues Stark Forecast — Bitcoin May Fall As Low As $10,000 This Year
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) faces a stark downside risk that could send prices below the previous bear market lows, according to a new analysis from blockchain data firm CryptoQuant. 

The firm warns that a confluence of geopolitical shocks, macroeconomic repricing, and fragile derivatives positioning could push the largest cryptocurrency as low as $10,000 in a worst‑case scenario — far beneath the last bear‑market trough near $15,000.

Political Shock From Trump Speech

CryptoQuant’s note comes against the backdrop of a substantial pullback from Bitcoin’s record highs. After peaking at roughly $126,000 last October, Bitcoin has retraced about 45% and has entered a months‑long consolidation range between $66,000 and $70,000. 

The firm highlights recent political developments as an immediate catalyst for the downside potential. CryptoQuant points to President Donald Trump’s April 1 speech on Iran as a market‑moving event that abruptly reset expectations. 

By signaling the possibility of intensified military action within the coming weeks, the speech undermined hopes for de‑escalation and prompted a broad risk‑off reaction. 

In CryptoQuant’s view, this was not merely a geopolitical scare — it forced a repricing of macro conditions that matter to risk assets like Bitcoin. 

As oil prices rise, inflationary pressures can return; a firmer dollar tightens dollar liquidity globally. CryptoQuant notes rising volatility — with the VIX near 25 — and widening Treasury spreads, both of which are symptomatic of deteriorating liquidity.

Three Possible Bitcoin Outcomes 

CryptoQuant lays out a range of possible outcomes. In a moderate stress event, the firm estimates Bitcoin could fall from the $70,000 area to roughly $50,000 — a 25–30% decline. 

If Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) outflows continue and spot demand remains soft, the medium‑term downside expands substantially, with prices potentially sliding into the $30,000–$20,000 range, representing declines of 60–70% from current levels. 

In the extreme scenario — for example, a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz or a sustained major conflict — global liquidity could seize up more completely. 

CryptoQuant suggests that in such circumstances, equities could plunge more than 30% and oil could spike to $150–$200 per barrel, conditions that could drive Bitcoin toward the $10,000 mark, an 85% drop from current trading prices.

Featured image from OpenArt, chart from TradingView.com

data.title: Avidbank Holdings, Inc. to Announce First Quarter 2026 Earnings Results After-Market on Monday, April 27, 2026
data.description:
SAN JOSE, CA / ACCESS Newswire / April 3, 2026 / Avidbank Holdings, Inc. ("Avidbank Holdings") announced today that it will release first quarter 2026 earnings results on Monday, April 27, 2026, after the market closes. Upon release, investors may access a copy of the earnings results on the Avidbank website at www.avidbank.com under About Us, Investor Relations.

Avidbank Holdings will host a conference call on Tuesday, April 28, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. (EDT) / 8:00 a.m. (PDT) to discuss its first quarter 2026 results. Investors may call in by dialing (800) 715-9871 within the US and +1(646) 307-1963 for all other locations. The conference ID number is 1715743. Participants may also pre-register for the conference by navigating to https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/983702195.

Alternatively, individuals may listen to a live webcast of the presentation by visiting the link on Avidbank's website at www.avidbank.com under About Us, Investor Relations. An audio replay of the live webcast is expected to be available by the evening of April 28, 2026, through the Investor Relations section of Avidbank's website.

About Avidbank

Avidbank Holdings, Inc. , headquartered in San Jose, California, offers innovative financial solutions and services. We specialize in commercial & industrial lending, venture lending, structured finance, asset-based lending, sponsor finance, fund finance, and real estate construction and commercial real estate lending. Avidbank provides a different approach to banking. We do what we say.

Contact:

Patrick T. Oakes

Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer

408-200-7390

IR@avidbank.com

SOURCE: Avidbank Holdings, Inc.

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Bitcoin Price To $80,000: How The February Bullish Trend Can Push It 20% Higher
data.description:
Crypto analyst Jordan has predicted that the Bitcoin price could rally to $80,000 in the short term. The analyst pointed to a February bullish trend that could spark this rally for the leading crypto. 

Bitcoin Price Eyes Rally To $80,000 Based On This Trend

In an X post, Jordan predicted that the Bitcoin price could rally to $80,000, citing a bullish trend that began in February. This was around when BTC formed a new local low of $60,000. Since then, the leading crypto has rebounded to as high as $76,000. The analyst noted that BTC has bounced every time the price has tested support in the lower $60,000 range. 

Jordan said that if the Bitcoin price can hold this level, then there could be a momentum push towards the $80,000 to $84,000 CME gap. He added that it is interesting that the price has remained above key support levels despite the U.S.-Iran war. Crypto analyst Doctor Profit also indicated that BTC could rally above $80,000 in the short term. 

In an X post, he stated that he will look to enter new shorts between $79,000 and $84,000 if the Bitcoin price revisits that zone. He further remarked that he sees a high medium probability that BTC will reach this zone. However, he added that, given the geopolitical situation with the war in Iran, he doesn’t think the risk-reward is worth it to go long in hopes that BTC will rally above $80,000. 

Doctor Profit also reiterated that the Bitcoin price is in a bear market and that the price hasn’t bottomed yet. As such, he believes that placing short orders between $79,000 and $84,000 is a much safer bet with targets below $50,000. 

Not Yet Time To Buy BTC

Crypto analyst CrypFlow stated that this is not yet the time to buy BTC, as the Bitcoin price has not yet bottomed. He noted that the 2-month stochastic RSI bullish cross is one signal that has consistently marked the best buying opportunities every cycle. The analyst explained that under this pattern, momentum resets below 20, sentiment turns negative, and a bullish cross later confirms the shift. 

CrypFlow further remarked that the cross marked the start of the bull run in the 2015, 2019, and 2023 cycles. However, that cross has yet to happen this time around. He noted that the stochastic RSI is resetting again and that the setup is building, but that the signal hasn’t triggered, signaling that the Bitcoin price could still drop lower. 

At the time of writing, the Bitcoin price is trading at around $66,800, up in the last 24 hours, according to data from CoinMarketCap.

data.title: R C M Technologies Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• R C M Technologies Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 77 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 37 cents. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 62 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 58 cents to 66 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 12.4% to $86.48 million from a year ago; analysts expected $84.46 million.

  • R C M Technologies Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 80 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $6.1 million.

  • R C M Technologies Inc shares had risen by 0.4% this quarter and lost 6.0% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the employment services peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for R C M Technologies Inc is $32.00, about 39.7% above its last closing price of $19.30

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 3 at 07:53 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: House Bill Would Ban More Chipmaking Equipment Shipments to China. One Company Would Feel the Most Pain. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Levine

A bipartisan group of U.S. representatives introduced a bill on Friday that would further tighten export controls for semiconductor manufacturing equipment sales to China. The whole equipment industry has something to lose were this bill to become law, but the hardest hit company would be ASML of the Netherlands.

The Multilateral Alignment of Technology Controls on Hardware (MATCH) Act seeks to close gaps in export controls and enforcement, which are a patchwork of executive branch regulations and negotiations that were implemented over years.

"The U.S. government has taken a number of steps to make sure that American companies are not providing critical technologies to China and trying to ensure that we maintain a lead in the AI supercomputing race, and we need to plug some gaps in some partner countries," Rep. Michael Baumgartner, a Republican from eastern Washington who introduced the bill, told Barron's.

The bill's 10 co-sponsors in the House are evenly split between the two parties. In the Senate, a similar bipartisan measure is expected to be introduced soon by Sens. Pete Ricketts (R.-Neb.) and Andy Kim, (D.-N.J.).

"Republicans and Democrats both look at China's activities that are destabilizing to global peace and security, and confrontational to American interests, and they have concerns," Baumgartner says.

Were the bill to become law in its current form, the most impacted of the big semiconductor manufacturing equipment makers would be the industry giant, ASML.

It is the leader in lithography machines, which are central to chipmaking, and are specifically called out in the bill. ASML has long been banned from selling its highest-end-machines to China, and the newest product ASML can currently sell was launched in 2015. This bill would ban sales of that machine and others in the ASML lineup.

The bill would also prevent some high-margin services that the equipment-makers provide their Chinese customers.

ASML did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Despite not being able to sell its most expensive equipment that can cost up to 350 million euro ($403 million) per machine, ASML's sales to China have skyrocketed since China ramped up its semiconductor investment in the last few years. Only 14% of ASML system sales were to China in 2022. Even as export controls tightened, in 2024 Chinese sales were 41% of the total and then a third of sales in 2025.

During its fourth-quarter earnings call in January, the company said it expects Chinese sales to comprise only 20% of 2026 sales, though it made a similar prediction about 2025 that did not pan out.

The most crucial chips for artificial-intelligence computing, like Nvidia's GPU chips and high-bandwidth memory from Micron, are made on production lines employing ASML's high-end machines. Chinese manufacturers have been cut off from making these sorts of chips but have worked hard to make the older equipment more effective. It has also been producing lower-end chips in greater numbers, with much of them sold to domestic electronics manufacturers.

While not as important as the cutting edge chips from U.S., Korean and Taiwanese companies, the chips that are the target of the bill are used in AI data centers and military hardware. The proposed legislation aims to fight off any nascent Chinese advantage in those market segments.

Write to Adam Levine at adam.levine@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
